affected_pattern: Accounts whose certificate-based inter-service auth just expired show $0
root_cause: Expired mTLS certificate between billing and provisioning services — rate-lookup calls began failing silently (caught exception → default=0.0) after the cert expired at midnight UTC, so rating defaulted to zero for all downstream lookups.
supporting_evidence: Zero-Billing RCA Playbook: Inter-Service Auth Failure pattern
Expired cert CN=billing-to-provisioning with NotAfter matching failure window
Exception log 'SSLHandshakeException' with swallowed stack trace in RateLookupClient
recommended_action: Renew and rotate the inter-service certificate
Restart rating-engine instances to pick up the new cert
Replace silent exception catch with fail-closed behavior and explicit alert
Onboard the cert to the auto-renewal pipeline with 30-day expiry alerting
